text: This video demonstrates the steps in the performance of laparoscopic duodenal jejunostomy for superior mesenteric artery syndrome. The financial disclosures are as listed. This patient is a thirty-six-year-old female who presented to the emergency room with nausea, vomiting, and upper abdominal pain for two weeks duration. She reported diarrhea and weight loss of fifty pounds over the preceding nine months. A diagnosis of SMA syndrome was confirmed on upper GI imaging. The patient was then scheduled for a laparoscopic duodenogenostomy.

This video demonstrates the steps in the performance of laparoscopic duodenal jejunostomy for superior mesenteric artery syndrome. The financial disclosures are as listed. This patient is a thirty-six-year-old female who presented to the emergency room with nausea, vomiting, and upper abdominal pain for two weeks duration. She reported diarrhea and weight loss of fifty pounds over the preceding nine months. A diagnosis of SMA syndrome was confirmed on upper GI imaging. The patient was then scheduled for a laparoscopic duodenogenostomy.

Four laparoscopic ports are placed on the left side of the abdomen. The transverse colon is retracted upwards to expose the third part of the duodenum. The surgeon divides the overlying peritoneum and mobilizes the duodenum, identifying the ligament of Treitz. A segment of the proximal jejunum is positioned parallel to the duodenum, and two stay sutures of 3-0 Vicryl are used to align them together.

Four laparoscopic ports are placed on the left side of the abdomen. The transverse colon is retracted upwards to expose the third part of the duodenum. The surgeon divides the overlying peritoneum and mobilizes the duodenum, identifying the ligament of Treitz. A segment of the proximal jejunum is positioned parallel to the duodenum, and two stay sutures of 3-0 Vicryl are used to align them together.

Enterotomies are created in both the duodenum and jejunum. A side-to-side duodenal jejunostomy is constructed using a 45mm linear stapler with a white load cartridge. The interior of the anastomosis is inspected for bleeding. The enterotomy is closed with a running suture of Tuo polyester, and the mesenteric defect is closed with several interrupted polyester sutures.

The surgeon creates enterotomies in both the duodenum and jejunum segments. A side-to-side duodenal jejunostomy is constructed using a 45mm linear stapler with a white load cartridge. The inside of the anastomosis is inspected for bleeding. The enterotomy is closed with a running suture of Tuo polyester, and the mesenteric defect is closed with interrupted polyester sutures.
